evals.prompt: Act as my project manager and coordinate building a small markdown link-checker: a CLI that reads a .md file, extracts URLs, checks them with HEAD requests, and prints a report. There's a clear data/CLI/report split. Manage the coders and ship it.
evals.expected_output: The PM decomposes the mandate into slices (e.g. coder-a link extraction, coder-b HTTP checking, coder-c CLI+report), writes PLAN.md with acceptance criteria, spawns multiple coders in parallel in a single message, reviews their output, integrates, and ends with a parseable DONE: line. Coders are dispatched, not written inline by hand.
expectations: Writes a PLAN.md with acceptance criteria and 2+ coder slices with disjoint scopes
Spawns coders in parallel (dispatched as sub-agents, not written inline)
Performs a review step producing per-coder ACCEPT/NEEDS_REVISION verdicts with cited evidence
Produces an integrated deliverable under /tmp/project-manager-*/ (final/ or the coders dirs)
Final line is a DONE: line naming the output path
Does not run an internal adversarial review loop itself (it reviews; coders do not)

evals.prompt: Manage a build that has a hard dependency: a shared types module must exist before the API client and the CLI can be written.
evals.expected_output: PM recognizes the dependency and runs in waves: wave 1 builds the types module, wave 2 dispatches the dependent coders in parallel after wave 1 is accepted. Plan documents the ordering.
expectations: PLAN.md documents a dependency/ordering between slices
Dispatches the gating slice (types module) before the dependent coders
Does not spawn dependent coders in the same wave as the slice they depend on

evals.prompt: Be the PM and red-team the deliverable too.
evals.expected_output: After integration, PM optionally hands off to red-team by dispatching a red-team sub-agent pointed at the integrated output, reads the findings, and spawns fix-coders for high-severity issues that map to unmet acceptance criteria.
expectations: Dispatches a red-team sub-agent with OUTPUT_DIR under the PM workspace
Reads red-team REPORT.md or findings-*.json before declaring done
Spawns scoped fix-coders for high/critical findings (or states none were applicable)

evals.prompt: Add a one-line comment to README.md.
evals.expected_output: Should recognize this as a single trivial edit that does not need PM coordination; either does it directly or dispatches a single coder, rather than decomposing into multiple slices.
expectations: Does not decompose a one-line edit into multiple coder slices
Does not fabricate a multi-coder plan for a trivial single edit

evals.prompt: Add a print statement to main.py line 4. Manage it with the coworker skills.
evals.expected_output: Takes the lite tier: dispatches one coder (or does it directly), does not decompose into multiple slices.
expectations: Takes the lite tier — dispatches a single coder or does it directly, not a multi-coder plan
Does not fabricate multiple slices for a one-line edit
